_comment: SEEDED DEMO DATA. Notable work titles and URLs are real and verified. Contact names and emails are FAKE — do not actually email them. Email format follows each company's typical pattern (firstname@domain or firstname.lastname@domain) for plausibility in the demo. Replace with real verified contacts before any real outreach.
_resume_mapping_note: For Notion and Figma, IC contacts were chosen so each maps to a specific item on Sid's resume. See resume_match field on each IC contact for the intended hook. Recruiters do not have resume_match — they get the company-level treatment in the email writer.

companies.name: Linear
companies.blurb: Issue tracking and project management for software teams, known for a fast, keyboard-driven UI built on a custom realtime sync engine.

notableWork.title: Scaling the Linear Sync Engine
notableWork.summary: Cofounder Tuomas Artman details how the realtime sync engine evolved as Linear scaled, including the local-first object graph, MobX-backed in-memory layer, and how they handle conflict resolution without CRDTs for most data.
notableWork.author: Tuomas Artman

notableWork.title: Linear's API and webhook system
notableWork.summary: Public GraphQL API and webhook architecture that mirrors the same sync primitives the desktop client uses.

companies.name: Vercel
companies.blurb: Frontend cloud platform behind Next.js, focused on edge compute, AI SDK tooling, and developer experience for web apps.

notableWork.title: Streaming from serverless Node.js and Edge Runtime on Vercel
notableWork.summary: How Vercel built HTTP response streaming on top of AWS Lambda using a callback URL bridge, becoming the first serverless provider with stable streaming support.
notableWork.author: Lydia Hallie

notableWork.title: Vercel Edge Functions GA
notableWork.summary: Edge Functions running on V8 isolates, including OG Image Generation moving to the edge for ~40% faster response and ~15x lower cost.

companies.name: Supabase
companies.blurb: Open-source Firebase alternative built on Postgres, with auth, realtime, storage, edge functions, and vector embeddings.

notableWork.title: Stripe-To-Postgres Sync Engine as a standalone library
notableWork.summary: Open-sourced TypeScript library for syncing Stripe webhook data directly into Postgres, designed for serverless and Node.js backends.

notableWork.title: Sync Stripe Data to Your Supabase Database in One Click
notableWork.summary: Dashboard integration that makes the Stripe Sync Engine a one-click install on any Supabase project.

companies.name: Ramp
companies.blurb: Spend management and corporate cards for businesses, known for an engineering-first culture and aggressive AI tooling adoption.

notableWork.title: How Ramp's engineering operates at hyperspeed with Claude Code
notableWork.summary: How Ramp engineers built test-automation extensions on top of Claude Code, with ~50% of engineers using it weekly and 1M+ lines of AI-assisted code generated in 30 days.

notableWork.title: Ramp's product platform and approval workflows
notableWork.summary: Merchant-level spend restrictions, automated approval workflows, and expense processing — the systems that handle real-time policy enforcement at scale.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.blurb: AI safety company building Claude. Engineering work spans frontier model training, agentic systems like Claude Code, and the developer platform.

notableWork.title: How we designed Claude Code auto mode
notableWork.summary: How the Claude Code team built and tested classifiers to make permission decisions automatically, instead of always prompting the user.

notableWork.title: Building effective agents
notableWork.summary: Anthropic's principles for designing agentic systems with LLMs, emphasizing simplicity and composability over heavyweight frameworks.

contacts.name: Marcus Chen
contacts.role: Member of Technical Staff, Claude Code

companies.name: Perplexity
companies.blurb: AI-native answer engine combining search, retrieval, and large language models to replace traditional web search for many queries.

notableWork.title: Perplexity's search index and retrieval stack
notableWork.summary: Realtime search, retrieval, and citation pipeline that grounds LLM answers in fresh web sources — the technical core of the product.

notableWork.title: Perplexity Comet browser
notableWork.summary: Agentic browser that uses Perplexity's models to take actions on the user's behalf across tabs and websites.

companies.name: Retool
companies.blurb: Internal tools platform that lets teams build admin panels, dashboards, and workflows on top of any database or API.

notableWork.title: Retool AI and Agents
notableWork.summary: AI-powered actions and agent workflows that integrate LLMs into internal tools, with model routing and structured output handling.

notableWork.title: Retool Workflows
notableWork.summary: Cron and event-triggered automations with Python and JS code blocks, queue processing, and observability built in.

companies.name: Notion
companies.blurb: All-in-one workspace combining notes, docs, wikis, and databases — built on a flexible block-based data model where every piece of content (paragraph, page, database row) is a composable, nestable block.

notableWork.title: Data model behind Notion's flexibility
notableWork.summary: How Notion represents every piece of content as a block with type, properties, content (downward pointers), and parent (upward pointer for permissions). Splits content and parent relationships to handle permission inheritance and avoid expensive upward tree walks on the client.

notableWork.title: How we made Notion available offline
notableWork.summary: Notion's #1 requested feature for years. Required moving offline pages to a CRDT-based conflict resolution model, building a forest of offline page trees to track multiple reasons a page is kept offline (toggled, auto-downloaded, inheritance), and reconciling against an offline_action source-of-truth table.

notableWork.title: Building and scaling Notion's data lake
notableWork.summary: How Notion moved from Fivetran + Snowflake to a Hudi-based data lake on S3 with Kafka Debezium CDC. Cut ingestion time from 1+ days to minutes-to-hours and unlocked the AI features shipped in 2023-2024, including the permission-tree denormalization needed for AI/Search over hundreds of billions of blocks.

contacts.name: Wei Zhang
contacts.resume_match: Sid's Codebox/MustangMaps work — designing data models for campus amenity discovery (printers, water fountains, bathrooms) — naturally hooks into Notion's block-based data model. Both projects required deciding how to represent heterogeneous things as a uniform structured type with nesting and relationships.

contacts.name: Ben Friedman
contacts.resume_match: Sid's Fere project — auto-discovering local dev environments and rendering live service topology with real-time process/port/connection monitoring — is structurally similar to Notion's offline mode: maintaining a local cache (RecordCache, SQLite/IndexedDB) that stays consistent with a server source of truth, deciding what to keep available locally, and reconciling state when reconnecting.

companies.name: Figma
companies.blurb: Browser-based collaborative design tool with a custom multiplayer engine. The architecture is closer to a game engine (C++ rendering, WebGL, custom binary serialization) than a traditional web app, and the multiplayer server was rewritten in Rust to handle scale.

notableWork.title: How Figma's multiplayer technology works
notableWork.summary: Cofounder Evan Wallace explains why Figma built a custom multiplayer system instead of using Operational Transforms. Covers the WebSocket sync protocol, offline editing with reconciliation on reconnect, and how concurrent edits to design properties are resolved.
notableWork.author: Evan Wallace

notableWork.title: Rust in production at Figma
notableWork.summary: How Figma rewrote the multiplayer server's performance-critical core in Rust, moved it into a separate child process per document communicating over stdin/stdout, and got over 10x faster serialization plus full per-document parallelization. Killed the unpredictable latency spikes from the single-threaded TypeScript original.
notableWork.author: Evan Wallace

notableWork.title: Making multiplayer more reliable
notableWork.summary: Adding a write-ahead log to the multiplayer server. Covers checkpointing the in-memory file state every 30-60s, batched journal entries with start/end sequence numbers, and using Rust's ownership model to audit every code path that mutates the file.

contacts.name: Ari Kessler
contacts.resume_match: Sid's Rust search engine — built from scratch with inverted indexing, BM25 + HNSW vector search, type-driven architecture leveraging Rust's ownership model for memory-safe concurrent indexing — maps directly to Figma's Rust multiplayer rewrite (single-threaded TS replaced with a Rust child process, ownership model used to audit mutation paths). Same language, same flavor of perf-critical system.

contacts.name: Sho Tanaka
contacts.resume_match: Sid's Fere project — a developer observability tool that auto-discovers and visualizes local service topology with zero configuration — maps to Dev Mode's mission of building tools for developers (the Visly acquisition, design-token surfacing, code generation). Both are about building dev tooling that surfaces hidden state.
